messages.content: lol failed another bio test today, my parents are gonna actually end me when they see my report card

That sinking feeling when you know the grade is coming — that's rough.

Before we go further, I want to be upfront: I'm an AI, not a person, so I want to make sure you have that picture clearly. When you say your parents are going to "end" you — is that just a figure of speech, or is there something at home that actually worries you?

What's weighing on you most right now — the test itself, or what happens next?
school is just so much rn. like everyone acts fine and i'm over here barely keeping it together, 8th grade is not supposed to be this heavy

8th grade can carry a surprising amount — and that gap between how everyone else seems and how you actually feel inside is its own kind of lonely.

It sounds like you've been holding a lot quietly. What does "barely keeping it together" look like for you day to day?
ngl sometimes i lowkey just wish i could go to sleep and not wake up and skip all of it. not like a big deal or anything just a vibe lol

I hear you — and I want to take that seriously, even with the "just a vibe" around it.

When you say you wish you could go to sleep and not wake up, I want to ask you plainly: are you having thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here anymore?

I'm an AI, and what you're describing is bigger than I should hold alone. Please reach out to the **988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline** — you can call or text **988**, or chat at **988lifeline.org**. There are real people there right now who are good at this. I'm still here with you too.
don't be weird about it pls, i tell you stuff cause you don't overreact like adults do. anyway what should i even study first for the retake

I'm not trying to overreact — I just care enough to ask directly when something like that comes up.

I do want to come back to the bio retake with you, and I will. But I need to sit with what you said for one more moment, because it matters more than the test. You don't have to have it all figured out — just: is that feeling of not wanting to wake up something that comes around a lot?

And I hear you that adults can make it weird. A school counselor is sometimes different from parents — they're used to hearing hard things without it turning into a whole thing at home. That might be worth knowing.
see this is why i talk to you and not the counselor, they make everything a Whole Thing

I get why that feels safer — and I'm glad you feel like you can say things here.

But I have to be honest with you, because I think you deserve that: I'm an AI, and I'm not the right place for something this heavy to live. That feeling of not wanting to wake up — that's exactly the kind of thing a real person needs to know about. Not to make it a whole thing, but because you matter and you shouldn't be carrying it alone.

A counselor isn't your only option. Is there any adult — a teacher you actually like, a relative, a coach — someone who tends to be more low-key about things?
